products.menuLine: 1792 Small Batch Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 93.7 proof (46.9% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: caramel, vanilla

guidance.avoidIf: User wants wheated or very mild.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when cinnamon and pepper should echo a spicy cigar — high-proof without Booker's price.
guidance.memberBlurb: 1792 Small Batch — cinnamon and pepper over caramel at nearly 94 proof. Value bourbon that still feels serious beside a Corojo.

products.menuLine: Four Roses Single Barrel · Kentucky · $45 · 100 proof (50.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: cherry, spice

guidance.bestFor: Bourbon nerd, single-barrel interest, spice-forward cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend for high-rye fruit and spice beside a medium-full cigar — cherry and baking spice without peat.
guidance.memberBlurb: Four Roses Single Barrel — cherry and spice from a high-rye recipe. The bourbon pour that earns respect from enthusiasts without collector hype.

products.menuLine: Aberlour 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $55 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: sherry, black cherry

guidance.bestFor: Sherry lover, cherry-chocolate cigar, Speyside value.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when sherry sweetness should meet a maduro — black cherry, chocolate, toffee.
guidance.memberBlurb: Aberlour 12 — sherry, black cherry, and chocolate in a Speyside pour. Sherry depth without only reaching for Macallan.

products.menuLine: Angel's Envy Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $55 · 86.6 proof (43.3% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: port wine, cocoa

guidance.bestFor: Member wants finished bourbon, cocoa-leaning cigar, conversation pour.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants dry or unpeated only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when port sweetness and cocoa in the glass should echo a maduro — raisin, vanilla, and oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Angel's Envy — port-finished bourbon with cocoa and raisin over caramel. The finished bourbon cigar lounges keep coming back to.

products.menuLine: Appleton Estate 21 Year · Jamaica · $120 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: orange, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Rum collector, funky Jamaican ask, celebration cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants Jamaican character — orange, oak, and noble funk.
guidance.memberBlurb: Appleton Estate 21 — orange and vanilla over Jamaican funk and oak. Luxury rum with personality beyond the sweet shelf.

products.menuLine: Ardbeg 10 Year · Islay, Scotland · $55 · 92 proof (46.0% ABV) · Notes: peat smoke, lime

guidance.bestFor: Smoke maximalist, Lagavulin wasn't enough, bold maduro.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants the peatiest pour on the rail — lime, espresso, and charcoal smoke.
guidance.memberBlurb: Ardbeg 10 — lime and espresso riding a wave of peat. For the member who told you Lagavulin was too subtle.

products.menuLine: Balvenie 14 Year Caribbean Cask · Speyside, Scotland · $75 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: toffee, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Finished Scotch curious, rum-cask interest, celebratory pour.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants dry or smoky only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when rum-cask sweetness should complement a medium-full cigar — toffee, vanilla, apple.
guidance.memberBlurb: Balvenie 14 Caribbean Cask — toffee and rum sweetness from a Speyside finish. Scotch that borrows rum's friendship with cigars.

products.menuLine: Basil Hayden's Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: peppermint, tea

guidance.bestFor: Mild cigar, first bourbon, user intimidated by proof.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants cask-strength or heavy flavor.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants a gentle bourbon beside a mild cigar — peppermint, honey, and light spice.
guidance.memberBlurb: Basil Hayden's — light, peppery, and approachable. The bourbon for a Connecticut wrapper when 100+ proof feels like too much.
